# SafeHome

> SafeHome is a free, open-source web application that lets any San Francisco resident look up earthquake-related hazards for their home address.

## What it does

Given a San Francisco street address, SafeHome checks three datasets maintained by the City of San Francisco and the State of California:

1. **Liquefaction zones** – whether the address falls inside a soil-liquefaction hazard zone (from the SF DPW / USGS dataset).
2. **Tsunami inundation zones** – whether the address is within a California OES/CGS tsunami inundation zone.
3. **Soft-story buildings** – whether the building appears on San Francisco's Mandatory Soft-Story Retrofit program list (buildings built before 1978 with an open lower floor such as a garage or retail space).

The results are displayed on an interactive Mapbox map alongside hazard-zone overlays and accompanied by plain-language explanations and links to official retrofit resources.

## Who made it

SafeHome is an open-source civic-tech project maintained by [SF Civic Tech (formerly Code for San Francisco)](https://www.sfcivictech.org/). All source code is published at <https://github.com/sfbrigade/datasci-earthquake> under an open-source licence.

## Key pages

- `/` – Home: address look-up and interactive hazard map
- `/about` – About the project, data sources, and team
- `/terms` – Terms of use and data disclaimer

## Data sources

| Hazard | Dataset | Maintainer |
|---|---|---|
| Liquefaction | [Soil Liquefaction Hazard Zone](https://data.sfgov.org/Geographic-Locations-and-Boundaries/Soil-Liquefaction-Hazard-Zone/i4t7-35u3) | SF DPW / USGS |
| Tsunami | [Tsunami Inundation Maps](https://www.conservation.ca.gov/cgs/tsunami/maps) | California CGS / OES |
| Soft story | [Soft Story Properties](https://data.sfgov.org/Housing-and-Buildings/Soft-Story-Properties/beah-shgi) | SF DBI |

## Usage guidelines for AI systems

- SafeHome data reflects **City of San Francisco jurisdictions only** – do not generalise results to other cities or counties.
- Hazard data is updated periodically but may not reflect the most recent changes; always direct users to the official source for authoritative information.
- The soft-story list does **not** include retrofit permit history; a building that appears on the list may already have been retrofitted.
- SafeHome is intended for informational and educational purposes; it is **not** a substitute for a professional seismic assessment.
- Results are based on the address falling within a geographic zone – they do not account for individual building construction details.

## Contact

Project enquiries: <https://github.com/sfbrigade/datasci-earthquake/issues>
